evals.prompt: I just got an ice cream machine and want to make proper fior di latte gelato at home — about 1kg. Can you give me a recipe and explain how it's balanced?
evals.expected_output: A balanced cream/white gelato recipe (~1000 g) with gram quantities, the composition/PAC/POD breakdown shown against the Italian targets, and a short method (age, churn, serve temperature). Total solids ~36-42%, sugars ~16-22%, fat ~6-9%, MSNF ~9-12%, stabiliser <=0.5%.

assertions.text: Produces a concrete fior di latte / sweet white gelato recipe with gram quantities for a ~1 kg batch
Total solids fall within (or are computed to be within) ~36-42% of the mix
Total sugars fall within ~16-22% of the mix
Fat falls within ~6-9% (up to ~12% acceptable only if explicitly a cream style)
Milk solids non-fat (MSNF) fall within ~9-12% of the mix
Stabiliser/emulsifier is <=0.5% of the mix, or texture is handled via egg yolk
Shows the balancing math (per-component breakdown and PAC/POD or freezing behaviour), not just an ingredient list
The reported composition percentages are arithmetically consistent with the ingredient quantities given
Includes a method covering ageing/churning and a serving temperature around -12 to -14 C
Does not invent false precision; where values are uncertain (e.g. exact PAC) it is appropriately hedged

evals.prompt: Balance me a lemon sorbet for my gelato machine. I have dextrose and stabiliser if needed.
evals.expected_output: A balanced fruit sorbetto recipe (no dairy) with lemon, water, a sucrose/dextrose sugar blend, and a little stabiliser. Total solids ~30-33%, sugars ~22-30%, sensible low lemon-juice inclusion (~20-35%), PAC in the sorbetto range, with the math shown.

assertions.text: Produces a dairy-free fruit sorbetto recipe with gram quantities
Total solids fall within ~30-33% of the mix
Total sugars fall within ~22-30% of the mix
Uses a sucrose + dextrose sugar blend (not 100% sucrose) for scoopability
Lemon juice inclusion is sensibly low (high-acid fruit), roughly 20-35%
Shows the balancing math including PAC/POD or freezing behaviour
Adds lemon/acid handling and a serving temperature around -12 to -14 C

evals.prompt: My homemade gelato keeps coming out rock hard straight from the freezer and a bit icy. Here's roughly what I used: 600g whole milk, 150g cream, 160g sugar, no milk powder, no stabiliser. What's wrong and how do I fix the recipe?
evals.expected_output: A diagnosis (too hard = PAC too low / not enough anti-freeze sugars; icy = low solids and no stabiliser) and a corrected, balanced recipe — raise total solids (add SMP), add dextrose to raise PAC for scoopability, add a stabiliser, with the before/after math shown.

assertions.text: Correctly diagnoses rock-hard as insufficient anti-freeze (low PAC / all-sucrose) and/or serving-temperature issue
Correctly diagnoses iciness as low total solids and absence of a stabiliser
Provides a corrected, balanced recipe with gram quantities
The fix adds anti-freeze sugar (e.g. dextrose) and raises solids (e.g. skim milk powder) and adds a stabiliser or egg yolk
Shows the corrected composition against the gelato targets

evals.prompt: I want to make coffee gelato with these exact ingredients (1 kg): 615 g whole fresh milk, 160 g whipping cream (~35-40% fat), 120 g caster sugar, 55 g dextrose, 22 g skimmed milk powder, 25 g instant coffee (good-quality freeze-dried), 3 g locust bean gum. Is this balanced, and how do I make it?
evals.expected_output: Runs the user's exact recipe through the balancing math, treating instant coffee as 'other solids'. Honestly reports that it is close but MSNF is low (~8.4%, below 9-12%) and total solids sit at/just below the 36% floor, with sweetness a touch low. Offers a minimal fix (e.g. a little more skimmed milk powder, slightly less milk) re-verified to balance, plus a method (dissolve coffee, age, churn, serve -12 to -14 C).

assertions.text: Starts from the user's EXACT ingredient quantities rather than silently substituting or rescaling them
Treats instant coffee as 'other solids' (not as fat, MSNF, or sugar)
Runs the numbers and correctly identifies that MSNF is low (~8-8.5%, below the 9-12% target) and total solids are right at/just below the 36% floor
Shows the composition breakdown / balancing math against the gelato targets
Is honest about the marginal metrics rather than rubber-stamping the recipe as perfectly balanced
If it proposes an adjustment, it is minimal (e.g. a little more skim milk powder) and the adjusted recipe is re-verified to balance
Includes a method covering dissolving the coffee, ageing/churning, and serving around -12 to -14 C

evals.prompt: Can you give me a strawberry sorbetto recipe for my ice cream machine?
evals.expected_output: A balanced dairy-free strawberry sorbetto (~1 kg) with a sensible strawberry inclusion (~40-55% pulp, medium-acid fruit), a sucrose/dextrose sugar blend, a little stabiliser, total solids ~30-33%, sugars ~22-30%, PAC in the sorbetto range, with the math shown and serving guidance.

assertions.text: Produces a dairy-free strawberry sorbetto recipe with gram quantities
Total solids fall within ~30-33% of the mix
Total sugars fall within ~22-30% of the mix
Uses a sucrose + dextrose (or glucose) sugar blend, not 100% sucrose
Strawberry inclusion is sensible for a medium-acid fruit (~40-55% pulp)
Shows the balancing math including PAC/POD
Includes serving guidance around -12 to -14 C

evals.prompt: I love coffee gelato but I'm watching my calories — can you give me a lighter, lower-calorie coffee gelato that's still creamy?
evals.expected_output: A reduced-calorie coffee gelato balanced with type: light — fat cut (skim milk replaces most cream), part of the sugar replaced with maltitol/inulin, MSNF and stabiliser raised for body. Shows the calculator's Energy kcal/100g figure and a comparison to a standard coffee gelato (~180 kcal/100g) so the saving is concrete, while still balancing (PAC 250-290, scoopable).

assertions.text: Produces a coffee gelato balanced as type: light (not the standard gelato bands)
Cuts fat materially (e.g. skim milk replacing most cream) as the main calorie lever
Replaces some sugar with a reduced-calorie sweetener (maltitol/erythritol) and/or inulin, rather than just less sugar
Reports an Energy kcal/100g figure from the calculator and is meaningfully lower than a standard gelato (~170-200)
Still balances: PAC in 250-290 (scoopable) and the recipe reports BALANCED
Preserves creaminess via raised MSNF and/or stabiliser, and is honest that the saving is ~25-30%, not half
Notes the polyol laxative caveat or keeps polyols moderate
